title: Deployment Discovery & Filtering
goal: Connect to the Azure AI Foundry resource using the initialized SDK client, retrieve all available model deployments, filter to only text-based inference models, and return a structured list of deployment metadata for use by the UI and inference engine.

in_scope: Use the AIProjectClient to list all deployments on the configured Foundry resource
Extract per-deployment metadata: deployment name, model name, model type (chat/completion), and SKU or version info
Filter out non-text-based deployment types (e.g., embedding, image, audio models)
Return a list of deployment data objects (dicts or dataclasses)
Handle the case where zero compatible deployments are found — return empty list with a descriptive reason
Handle SDK errors during discovery gracefully (network timeout, auth failure)

outputs: src/discovery.py — module with deployment discovery and filtering logic
List of deployment metadata dicts, each containing: deployment_name, model_name, model_type, raw metadata
dependencies: Part 02 — Initialized SDK client must be available
checklist: Create src/discovery.py with a discover_deployments(client) function
Call the appropriate SDK method to list all deployments from the Foundry resource
For each deployment, extract: deployment_name, model_name (if available, else 'unknown'), model_type
Define a list of compatible model types for text inference (e.g., 'chat', 'completion', 'text-generation')
Filter deployments to include only those with compatible model types
Return a list of dicts with keys: deployment_name, model_name, model_type
If no compatible deployments pass the filter, return an empty list
Wrap the SDK call in a try/except to catch network and authentication errors
Return a meaningful error message if the SDK call itself fails (distinct from empty results)
acceptance_criteria: discover_deployments() returns a list of dicts for valid deployments
Each dict contains at minimum: deployment_name, model_name, model_type
Non-text deployments (embedding, image, audio) are excluded from results
An empty Foundry resource returns an empty list without raising an exception
SDK network/auth errors are caught and re-raised as a descriptive exception
The function is importable and callable independently of Streamlit
review_focus: Filtering logic correctly identifies text-based inference models vs. non-compatible types
The SDK method used for listing deployments is correct for the azure-ai-projects package
Error handling differentiates between 'no deployments found' and 'SDK call failed'
Deployment metadata extraction handles missing/optional fields gracefully
risks_or_notes: The Azure AI Foundry SDK API for listing deployments may vary — verify the exact method name and return type
Model type classification may not be straightforward; some deployments may lack explicit type metadata
The SDK may paginate results — ensure all pages are consumed
Consider caching the deployment list in Streamlit session state to avoid re-fetching on every rerun
definition_of_done: Function returns correctly structured deployment metadata for text-based models
Empty resource scenario returns empty list without crash
SDK errors produce descriptive error messages
Function is testable with a mock client
